scenario: You work for an events venue which uses an on-premises application to produce concert tickets with QR codes. You have developed a canvas app, using Dataverse as a data source, which will allow staff members to scan the QR code on the ticket and record attendance at the venue. In order to synchronize the tickets sold to Dataverse, you develop a custom web application that will process a flat file of ticket details (which includes the contact details of the purchaser and the details of the ticket purchased, as extracted from the on-premises application on the night of the concert) and consume the Dataverse Web API to add the ticket details into Dataverse. For each row in the file, the app will either retrieve or create Dataverse Contact details, then it will use a POST request to create a new row in the custom Ticket table and associate it with the correct Contact. A typical file contains hundreds of thousands of rows. After five minutes of testing in the User Acceptance Testing (UAT) environment, the Web API begins to return 429 Errors. You update your error handling to capture the Retry-After header in the response and you update the functions to respect this value.
q: Which two actions should you perform to mitigate future errors? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: Update the UI to prevent application users from submitting more requests before the previous request is completed.
Update the number of seconds delay to the value returned by the Retry-After header as a hardcoded value.
Determine the maximum number of requests within a five-minute window, then deploy the application to the production environment using the same request configuration.
Update your application so that it groups requests into batches.
Update the UI so that any error messages returned by the Dataverse Web API are displayed to the application users.
answer: Update the UI to prevent application users from submitting more requests before the previous request is completed.
Update your application so that it groups requests into batches.
why: The 429 errors are Dataverse service-protection limits (too many requests). To reduce the number of calls, group the many operations into BATCH requests ($batch), which sends multiple operations in a single call and dramatically lowers the request count. Also prevent parallel/overlapping submissions by updating the UI so a user cannot fire a new request before the previous one completes, keeping concurrent requests within limits. Hardcoding the Retry-After delay is wrong (the value varies), and assuming a fixed max-requests configuration or just displaying errors does not mitigate the throttling.
